content: If you are in an account other than the default and click to write a message, Thunderbird assume you want it to be from that account, not the default. The default is used when it would otherwise not be specific, such as when working in local folders.

content:
Thanks. I was afraid of that.

I would argue that that's not the right default. If I'm in an account, and am interrupted by something requiring a new email, Thunderbird doesn't know what account that interruption should use. So I often inadvertently shoot off a personal inquiry from a work email. (Or vice versa, but misusing a work email is worse IMO.)

If the new email is addressed to someone in my address book or a known domain, the Correct Identity add-on does a fantastic job of selecting the right identity. But it doesn't work for new messages.

I'll request that as a feature for the add-on.

content:
If you're composing a message in plain text format, you can use alt codes, where you hold <Alt> and type a code on the number pad. Some common ones are

Alt 172 or Alt 0188 for 1/4 Alt 171 or Alt 0189 for 1/2 Alt 0190 for 3/4

etc. Google "Alt Codes" for more.

If you're composing in HTML format, choose from the Compose window menu, <Insert | Characters and Symbols... | Common Symbols> and choose from the list.

content:
The message may have been all about the changes occuring with Yahoo. So lets deal with Yahoo first. Recent updated info

  • https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-yahoo

I would suggest you completely remove any stored passwords for the 'Yahoo' accounts first so there will be no confusion.

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • Scroll down to Passwords
  • Click on 'Saved Passwords'
  • Click on 'show Passwords'
  • then Remove any line that mentions the 'Username' = Yahoo email account. You mau only have one that begins with 'oauth://...' but if you see others that start with imap://..or smtp:// for the same yahoo account then make sure they are all removed.
  • Click on 'Close'
  • Now Exit Thunderbird, wait a few moments for background processes to compete and the restart Thunderbird.

You should get a prompt from Yahoo to enter password

IF you have more than one Yahoo account or additional accounts that uses the yahoo server such as AOL. Eg: you have one yahoo and one aol OR two or more yahoo accounts then you need to alter the following preference first.

  • Settings > General
  • Scroll to bottom and click on 'Config Editor'
  • It opens in a new tab
  • In search type: oauth.use
  • look for : mailnews.oauth.usePrivateBrowse
  • toggle from 'false' to 'TRUE'
  • Exit Thunderbird, wait a few moments for background processes to compete and the restart Thunderbird.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
What is your computer operating system? I believe this is a system problem, not a Thunderbird problem.

In Windows, it sometimes happens that a window winds up off-screen, that is, it's there, but there's little or none of it available to grab with the mouse. The fix in this case, is to use Alt+Tab to bring the errant window into the foreground (even if it's not visible.) Then press Alt+Spacebar, which will open up a context menu, one of the options will be Move. You can activate that option with the keystrokes Alt+Spacebar, then "M". You should then see a 4-sided arrow which will let you use the arrow keys or mouse to maneuver the window back to a better position.

Se a volte funziona ed altre no, il problema lo dovresti segnalare a TIM.

PIÙ in generale comunque, Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)

content:
Are you talking about a Pop or Imap account ?

Do you have an Anti-Virus which is allowed to scan files etc when either downloading mail OR whenever a file is opened ? Advise: to rule out Anti-Virus causing delays - Access AV and set up an exception to not scan anything in your Thunderbird profile. Usually located here: C://Users/username/Appdata/Roaming/Thunderbird

Gmail can also be a pain for those using POP accounts. If you do not periodically access the webmail account via a browser and delete old mail and also previously deleted mail from the 'All Mail' server then gmail can be a nuisance and deliberately stall downloads. Note only do the following if using a POP account in Thunderbird because those emails in a POP account are a completely independent copy on your computer. Access the gmail webmail account and delete the old mail from 'All Mail'.

Do not attempt this if using an IMAP account as you will lose emails from Thunderbird because an IMAP account can only display whatever is on server.

content:
In Thunderbird (TB) go to Help > Troubleshooting Information > Profile Folder > Open Folder...then close TB. In the profile folder open the Mail folder and look for a folder named "pop.freeuk.com" (or similar), open it and delete the popstate.dat file. This file contains info about which messages have already been downloaded in TB. Now restart TB which should re-download all messages still on the server.

John said
I don't understand why there's so many because I thought I had set them to delete when I deleted them from Outlook (classic) which is my main desktop client.

Did you set up the same email address in Outlook as POP account too? Handling the same email address in different mail clients and/or on different PCs can become tricky if you don't use the IMAP protocol on all supports. Maybe your freeuk.com provider didn't propose IMAP in the last century but in the meantime we are in 2026...

John said
I didn't even know I had the ESR version and don't understand the advantage of it.

ESR (extended support release) versions get major updates once a year only ESR releases are maintained for more than a year, with point releases that coincide with regular Thunderbird releases. These point releases contain security upgrades and stability fixes. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-esr

content:
Nejčastější příčiny:

  • Disk plný → zkontroluj volné místo. Jedna složka v Thunderbirdu má praktický limit cca 4 GB (formát mbox). Nad tuto hranici hrozí chyby a ztráta dat.
  • Složka jen pro čtení → pravým tlačítkem na profil/složku ve Windows → Vlastnosti
  • Poškozený index složky → v Thunderbirdu pravým na složku → Vlastnosti → Opravit složku
  • Antivir blokuje soubory → zkus dočasně vypnout nebo přidat výjimku pro profil Thunderbirdu
  • Profil na síťovém disku / OneDrive → problém se zápisem, ideálně mít lokálně

Rychlý test: Zkus uložit zprávu do jiné složky (např. Lokální složky). Pokud to projde, je problém jen v konkrétní složce.

content:
Je pravdepodobné, že máte narušený profil Thunderbirdu. Vytvorte si NOVÝ PROFIL. Na vytvorenie nového profilu môžete použiť príkazy v ponuke Štart (WIN+R) a tu vypíšte: "thunderbird -p" - bez úvodzoviek a preverte, či problém pretrváva. Ak problém nepominie, tak sa môžete prepnúť späť k pôvodnému profilu tiež príkazom "thunderbird -p" http://imgbank.cz/images/2015/08/20/Thunderbird_novy_profil.jpg

Do NOVÉHO PROFILU si potom cez "Nástroje“ - „Importovať" - „Importovať z inej inštalácie Thunderbirdu“ importujte všetky účty a nastavenia, adresáre, e-mailové správy a kalendár ... zo STARÉHO (pôvodného) profilu Thunderbirdu - zvoľte si iba to čo potrebujete. https://imgbank.cz/images/2023/10/25/Thunderbird_import.jpg

Ak je problém odstránený, potom pokračujte v tvorbe (budovaní) NOVÉHO PROFILU Thunderbirdu. Nakoniec môžete ten STARÝ profil odstrániť.
